blocks.content: Thursday morning. The hallway near the gym. I heard it before I saw it.
Jonathan didn't stop walking. He slowed down — just enough to let Daniel think he had his attention.

blocks.speaker: Daniel
blocks.content: Then turn around when I'm talking to you.
Jonathan stopped. Turned around. And I watched something happen that I'd never seen before — he looked at Daniel the way you look at something that doesn't understand what it's standing next to.
Not angry. Not threatened. Genuinely amused, in a way that was somehow worse than either of those.
That was it. One word. And a look that said: I've already seen how this ends and it doesn't end well for you. I promise on God you don't want this.
Daniel held it for a second. Then he laughed — the kind that doesn't reach the eyes — and walked off like he'd been the one to decide it was over.
